title: Certificate validation must not be disabled
messageFormat: Do not accept all certificates; validate the certificate and chain before trusting the connection.
summary: Assigning a certificate-validation callback that always returns true turns off transport security: the connection will trust any certificate, including a forged or expired one, which opens the door to man-in-the-middle interception. The callback must inspect the certificate, the chain, and the policy errors and reject anything it cannot verify.
